title: Reinforcement Learning
description: Study of reinforcement learning theory and algorithms covering tabular methods, function approximation, policy gradient methods, and deep reinforcement learning. Topics include Markov Decision Processes, value iteration, policy iteration, Monte Carlo methods, temporal difference learning (Q-learning, SARSA), deep Q-networks (DQN), policy gradient (REINFORCE, PPO), and actor-critic methods. Students implement RL agents for games and control tasks. CS305 (Machine Learning) is the prerequisite providing foundations in neural networks, optimization, and model evaluation needed for deep RL methods.
outcomes: Formulate sequential decision problems as MDPs
Compare model-free and model-based RL approaches
Design RL solutions for real-world problems
